title: EV-II

{{Quote|The EV-IIs more than compensated for the personality flaws evident in the first generation EVs; I used to own a standard EV when I was running an automated bulk freighter. Shortly before I upgraded to an EV-II, my supervisory droid managed to mistreat the droid crew so badly that one of the more aggressive astromechs actually blew it out of the airlock.|Hyrum-12, on [[Cynabar's Droid Datalog]]|<ref name="Cynabar's Fantastic Technology" />}}
The '''EV-II''' was the second generation model of [[EV-series supervisor droid/Legends|EV-series supervisor droid]] that was introduced by [[MerenData/Legends|MerenData]] to remedy the personality flaws that plagued its predecessor. Costing 6,400 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], it stood 1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall and was equipped with an [[Audio sensor/Legends|auditory receptor]], a [[Broadband antenna/Legends|broadband broadcast antenna/receiver system]], a high frequency [[Binary/Legends|binary]] [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], [[Human/Legends|Human]] range [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]], and a [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]], as well as an advanced [[Personality matrix/Legends|personality matrix]].<ref name="Cynabar's Fantastic Technology" />

[[Hyrum-12]] acquired an EV-II after their first generation EV was [[Spacing/Legends|spaced]] by an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] that it mistreated. They were of the opinion that it more than compensated for the issues present in the original model.<ref name="Cynabar's Fantastic Technology" /> Despite this, the EV-series, including the EV-II and [[V-series droid supervisor/Legends|V-series]] replacement models, was a financial failure.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Droids">''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The EV-II was first mentioned in ''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]'', a [[1997]] [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] supplement written by [[Drew Campbell]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. It was depicted in a sketch by [[Johannes Huber]], which also doubled as an illustration of the first generation EV.<ref name="Cynabar's Fantastic Technology" />
